document.docAuthor: Mérida-Rivas, Jorge A., Hinojosa-Díaz, Ismael A., Ayala-Barajas, Ricardo, Pozo, Carmen & Vandame, Rémy
document.masterDocTitle: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Revision of carpenter bees of the subgenus Neoxylocopa Michener (Hymenoptera: Apidae) from Mexico and Mesoamerica

paragraph.treatmentCitationGroup:
Apis frontalis
Olivier, 1789: 64
. Holotype : locality unknown ( ♀ ). Type locality: French Guiana , Cayenne.

Xylocopa fasciata Lepeletier, 1841: 202
. Holotype : Museum Natural d’Histoire Naturelle Paris, France ( ♂ ). Type locality: Brazil , synonymy by
Smith (1874:284)
.

Xylocopa nitens .
Lepeletier, 1841: 176
. Holotype : locality unknown ( ♀ ). Type locality: French Guiana , Cayenne, current whereabouts unknown and presumed by Hurd (1978) to be lost, synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis quadrimaculata Meunier, 1890: 64
. Holotype : whereabouts unknown ( ♂ ). Type locality: Ecuador , Pichincha , Quito, synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa morio callichlora Cockerell, 1911: 287
. Holotype : American Museum of Natural History, New York , USA ( ♀ ). Locality: Peru , Piura , synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis var. coeruleomicans Enderlein, 1913: 158
. Holotype : Academie Polonaise des Sciences, Warsaw, Poland ( ♀ ). Type locality: Brazil , Espírito Santo , synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis var. viridimicans Enderlein, 1913: 158
. Holotype : Academie Polonaise des Sciences, Warsaw, Poland ( ♀ ). Type locality: Panama , Chiriquí , synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis fabricii Cockerell, 1926: 658
. Holotype : American Museum of Natural History, New York , USA ( ♀ ). Locality: French Guiana , Maroni, synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis roseata Cockerell, 1926: 658
. Syntypes : National Museum of Natural History, Washington , D.C., USA ( ♀ ). Type locality: Ecuador , synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis trinitatis Cockerell, 1926: 658
. Syntype : American Museum of Natural History, New York , USA ( ♂ ). Type locality: Trinidad and Tobago , synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)
.

Xylocopa frontalis var. obscuripennis Cockerell, 1949: 484
. Holotype : National Museum of Natural History, Washington , D.C., USA ( ♂ ). Type locality: Honduras , Zamorano, synonymy by
Hurd (1978:82)
.

Xylocopa frontalis var. purpureipennis Cockerell, 1949: 484
. Holotype : National Museum of Natural History, Washington D.C. , USA ( ♂ ). Type locality: Honduras , Zamorano, synonymy by
Hurd (1978:81)

Xylocopa ( Megaxylocopa ) frontalis , Hurd & Moure, 1963: 151
;
Minckley 1998: 151
.

subSubSection.paragraph: Diagnosis . Body length, female, 27.08 mm (24.83–28.28; n=5), male, 28.86 mm (26.22–31.67; n=5). Female: Head with two arc-shaped elevations at the sides of middle ocellus ( Figs. 12 and 13 ); middle ocellus located somewhat above upper margin of eyes; area between parapsidal line and mesoscutal disc with sparse punctures (separated by three or more times their diameter); wings dark brown with blue, violet o coppery iridescence ( Fig. 15 ); pubescence evenly black; integument of terga generally black (some specimens from Costa Rica , Panama and South America with reddish terga); T1 to T3 with dense minute shallow punctures on discal area, some punctures with diameter approximately twice that of the minute punctures; T2 to T4 with very minute hairs, T5 hair 0.86–3.24 (length respect MOD). Male: T1 to T6 yellow with black bands half or more the terga surface, T7 yellow; T2 to T5 with black and yellow hairs on lateral areas; T5 and T6 with long, dense black hairs ( Fig. 19 ); posterior tibia internal distal extreme with a projection longer than wide, with sharp apex rounded at the tip, anterior margin with broad rounded lobe ( Fig. 21 ); length of hairs on terga respect MOD, T2 0.13–0.16, T3 0.15–0.24, T4 0.22–0.27, T5 0.17–0.37; tip of the lateral process of the gonostylus ending broad, ellipsoidal ( Fig. 106 ).

Description . Female : Integument and pubescence generally black, although some specimens from Costa Rica , Panama and South America have reddish terga [as described originally by Olivier (1789) ]; wings dark brown with blue ( Fig. 15 ), violet o coppery iridescence; venation dark brown. Body length 27.08 mm (24.83–28.28; n=5); length of forewing 24.49 mm (23.61–25.63; n=5); head length 6.68 mm (6.18–7.07; n=5) intertegular distance 7.93 mm (7.46–8.47; n=5); width of T2 12.32 (11.5–013.07; n=5). Head : With two arc-shaped elevations arise at the sides of the middle ocellus; middle ocellus located somewhat above upper margin of eyes and slightly below level of lateral ocelli ( Figs. 12 and 13 ).
Mesosoma : Mesoscutal
disc unpuctate, between the parapsidal line and discal area with sparse punctures, separated by three or more times their diameter. Metasoma : T1 to T3 with dense minute shallow punctures on discal area; some punctures with diameter approximately twice that of the minute punctures; T4 and T5 with punctures slightly larger than in the preceeding terga, separated by three or more times their diameter; T6 with dense punctures. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 to T4 with very minute hairs, T5 0.86–3.24. Male : Yellowish-orange integument on head and discal area of scutum; pubescence yellowish-orange on head and mesosoma; hyaline wings with venation reddish brown; body length 28.86 mm (26.22 – 31.67; n=5); length of forewing 24.72 mm (24.32 – 25.43; n=5); head length 5.09 mm (4.94–5.37; n=5); intertegular distance 7.84 mm (7.80 – 8.21; n=5); width of T2 12.99 mm (12.59 – 13.56; n=5). Head : Labrum with a triangular area without punctures on the middle region; rest of labrum with scattered punctures; clypeus with dense punctures on the middle region of lower submargin; disc of clypeus without punctures; inferolateral and superior lateral regions with scattered punctures, separated by two to four times their diameter; lateral ocelli above the supraocular margin.
Mesosoma : Between
the parapsidal line and discal area with scattered punctures, separated by three or more times their diameter ( Fig. 20 ). Posterior tibia, anterior margin and anterior area of internal region with long pubescence; posterior margin with alternating long and short hairs; distal extreme of internal surface toward the posterior region with dense short pubescence; internal distal extreme with a projection longer than it is wide, with sharp apex rounded at the point: anterior margin of this projection with a broad rounded lobe ( Fig. 21 ). Metasoma : T1 to T6 yellow with black bands occupying half or more of their surface area; T7 yellow; lateral areas of T2 to T5 with black and yellow hairs; T5 and T6 covered with long dense black hairs ( Fig. 19 ), T1 with dense, long, plumose hairs, T5 with long hairs on lateral areas, T2 to T5 with very minute hairs on discal area. Length of hairs on terga respect MOD as follows: T2 0.13–0.16, T3 0.15–0.24, T4 0.22–0.27, T5 0.17–0.37. Genitalia : Distal angle of the ventral section of the gonostylus, acute ( Fig. 107 ); basal angle at the base of lateral process of the gonostylus, obtuse ( Fig. 106 ); tip of the lateral process ending broad, ellipsoidal ( Fig. 106 ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 11–15. Female of
X. ( N. ) frontalis
. 11. Frontal view; 12. Frontal projections (frontal view); 13. Frontal projections (dorsal view); 14. Lateral habitus; 15. Dorsal habitus.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Variation . Throughout its distribution, females of
X. frontalis
present vartiation in color of terga and wings iridescence; some specimens from Costa Rica have T1 and T2 with reddish integument. In specimens from Panama , T1 to T4 or T5 are reddish in the basal region or on to the middle of the terga. In some regions of South America, specimens with black metasoma or reddish bands on the terga are observed (
Lucia et al . 2014
). Wing iridescence varies greatly, specimens from Mexico and Central America show mainly violet iridescence while South America specimens have different iridescence pattern depending on the geographical region; many species and subspecies have been described based on wing iridescence variation, and have later been synonymized.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 16–21. Male of
X. ( N. ) frontalis
. 16. Frontal view; 17. Lateral habitus; 18. Dorsal habitus; 19. T5 a T7; 20. Parapsidal line; 21. Posterior tibia, internal region

Commentaries .
X. frontalis
may be confused with
X. fimbriata
and
X. nautlana
but may be distinguished from these by the middle ocellus that divides the two projections.
